mission.title: Install and Configure Dragonfly
mission.description: Dragonfly is a modern in-memory data store that serves as a drop-in replacement for Redis and Memcached, offering significantly improved performance and resource efficiency. It is ideal for applications requiring high throughput and low latency caching solutions. Key trade-offs include compatibility with existing Redis/Memcached APIs and reduced resource consumption.

steps.title: Add the Dragonfly Helm repository
steps.description:
This step adds the official Dragonfly Helm chart repository to your Helm configuration, allowing you to install Dragonfly easily.

```bash

helm repo add dragonfly https://dragonflydb.github.io/charts

helm repo update

```

> This command adds the Dragonfly chart repository.
> Updates the local Helm chart repository cache.

steps.title: Install Dragonfly CRDs
steps.description:
This step installs the Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs) required for Dragonfly to function correctly within your Kubernetes cluster.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/dragonflydb/dragonfly/v1.37.0/deploy/crds/dragonflydb_v1alpha1_dragonfly_crd.yaml

```

> This command installs the necessary CRDs.

steps.title: Install Dragonfly using Helm
steps.description:
This step installs the Dragonfly application in the specified namespace using the Helm chart.

```bash

helm install dragonfly dragonfly/dragonfly --namespace dragonfly --create-namespace --version 1.37.0

```

> Installs Dragonfly in the 'dragonfly' namespace.

steps.title: Create a Dragonfly instance
steps.description:
This step creates a Dragonfly custom resource to deploy an instance of Dragonfly.

```bash

kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: dragonflydb.io/v1alpha1
kind: Dragonfly
metadata:
  name: my-dragonfly
  namespace: dragonfly
spec:
  replicas: 3
EOF

```

> Creates a Dragonfly instance with 3 replicas.
commands:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: dragonflydb.io/v1alpha1
kind: Dragonfly
metadata:
  name: my-dragonfly
  namespace: dragonfly
spec:
  replicas: 3
EOF

steps.title: Verify the Dragonfly instance
steps.description:
This step checks the status of the Dragonfly instance to ensure it is running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get dragonfly -n dragonfly

kubectl get pods -n dragonfly

```

> Lists the Dragonfly instances in the 'dragonfly' namespace.
> Verifies that all Dragonfly pods are running.

uninstall.title: Uninstall Dragonfly
uninstall.description:
This step removes the Dragonfly installation and its associated resources. Be aware that this will result in data loss and orphaned resources such as persistent volumes and load balancers.

```bash

helm uninstall dragonfly --namespace dragonfly

kubectl delete namespace dragonfly

```

> Uninstalls the Dragonfly release.
> Deletes the Dragonfly namespace and all resources within it.

upgrade.title: Upgrade Dragonfly
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you backup your data. This step will upgrade Dragonfly to the latest version while ensuring minimal downtime.

```bash

kubectl get dragonfly my-dragonfly -n dragonfly -o yaml > dragonfly-backup.yaml

helm upgrade dragonfly dragonfly/dragonfly --namespace dragonfly --version 1.37.0

kubectl get dragonfly -n dragonfly

```

> Backup the current Dragonfly instance configuration.
> Upgrades Dragonfly to the specified version.
> Verifies that the Dragonfly instance is upgraded and running.
commands: kubectl get dragonfly my-dragonfly -n dragonfly -o yaml > dragonfly-backup.yaml

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to connect to Dragonfly'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may occur if the Dragonfly pods are not running or are in a crash loop.

**Fix:**
Check the pod status with 'kubectl get pods -n dragonfly' and describe any failing pods using 'kubectl describe pod <pod-name> -n dragonfly'.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Dragonfly instance not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the Dragonfly custom resource was not created or has been deleted.

**Fix:**
Ensure the Dragonfly instance is created with 'kubectl get dragonfly -n dragonfly'. If not, recreate it.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient resources'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the cluster does not have enough resources to schedule the Dragonfly pods.

**Fix:**
Check cluster resource availability with 'kubectl describe nodes' and adjust resource requests/limits in the Dragonfly spec if necessary.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'CRD validation failed'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may occur if the CRDs are not installed or are outdated.

**Fix:**
Reinstall the CRDs using 'kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/dragonflydb/dragonfly/v1.37.0/deploy/crds/dragonflydb_v1alpha1_dragonfly_crd.yaml'.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new caching algorithms and improved metrics collection.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No specific migration steps required for this version.

resolution.summary:
You should see output similar to:

NAME          READY   STATUS    RESTARTS   AGE
my-dragonfly   3/3     Running   0          5m

indicating that the Dragonfly instance is running correctly.
codeSnippets:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: dragonflydb.io/v1alpha1
kind: Dragonfly
metadata:
  name: my-dragonfly
  namespace: dragonfly
spec:
  replicas: 3
EOF

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running version 1.26 or higher with sufficient resources.
